channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Something for the Weekend – 13/06/26
item.content:encoded:
Did you think Summer Game Fest was done and dusted? Well, Nintendo didn’t get the memo, announcing and hosting their summer Nintendo Direct on Tuesday with a boat load of announcements. Let’s dig in.

In the News This Week

Catch up on all the ports, trailers and announcements from the Nintendo Direct here, but here’s our highlights from that and the rest of the week’s news.

  • The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake has been revealed for Switch 2
  • Final Fantasy Resonance brings an all new HD-2D game with classic turn-based action
  • Xenoblade Genesis is a brand new game in the Xenoblade series, arriving next year
  • Big Walk will stroll onto PC, PS5 and Switch 2 in August
  • Nintendo Switch Sports Resort brings another 12 games to Wuhu Island
  • Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave launches in September for Switch 2
  • Report – Major layoffs to hit Xbox as CEO claims it is “not in a healthy spot”
  • Xbox Game Pass lost “millions” of subscribers from 2025’s price hike
  • Xbox will still release multiplatform games, but expect more full exclusives like Gears of War: E-Day
  • Report – Ubisoft closes two studios with up to 380 job losses
  • Valor Mortis will now release in October, because September’s filled with GTA 6 dodgers
  • Kingdom Hearts Cloud Versions are going offline for Switch buyers

Games in Review & Featured Articles

Do you like your games all kinds of elastic? Well, check out Crushed In Time, from our small selection of reviews.

  • Crushed In Time – PC – 8/10
  • Midnight Saturn – PC – 7/10
  • NBA The Run – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 6/10

Adjacent to the reviews, we also had a batch of games in Early Access and Switch 2 ports. Dead As Disco is off to a promising start as a musical action game, while Rise of the Tomb Raider is a competent port, and Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Switch 2 Edition is a mouthful for a pretty tasty upgrade.

We also had previews of the Roman survival game Romestead, the prologue demo for 1666: Amsterdam, and Owlcat’s next 40K RPG, Dark Heresy. Rhythm Paradise Groove enchanted Reu with its quirky rhythm action, while I had a great time digging into Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy, which gives a very different style of action adventure.

After going hands on, we spoke to Resonance’s Lead Writer, Carol Ann Bañuls, and took Crushed In Time’s Associate Producer Freddi Malavasi to task over shipping yet another (deliberately) broken game.

Dom explored how Xbox’s shifting strategy has led to Marcus Fenix fighting in his civvies, and we’ll finish up for the week with What We Played featuring NBA The Run, 33 Immortals and Rune Dice

Trailer Park

Splatoon Raiders gets a new trailer packed with features

Orbitals brings its anime It Takes Two co-op action to Switch 2 in September

The Dragon’s Dogma 2: Dark Arisen expansion is coming to all platforms

Pikuniku 2 enters the third dimension in 2027

Your Achievements

What have you all been playing?

  • Andrewww checked in with his continuing journey through Banishers: Ghosts of New Eden – “It’s interesting, story and atmosphere are quite good. But, there are so many side quests and things to do, so that open world fatigue will definitely set in at some point.” But he’s also really looking forward to Alien: Isolation 2, which was announced last week.

That’s the round up for this week. Hope you have a great weekend (I’ll be watching the 24 Hours of Le Mans) and we’ll see you back here on Monday!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/13/something-for-the-weekend-13-06-26/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
